title: On regularity of solutions to the Navier--Stokes equation with initial data in BMO^{-1}

thm:1.summary: Any mild solution u in $X_T$ to NSE with $u_0$ in $bmo^{-1}$ belongs to C([0,T); $bmo^{-1})$ in the weak*-topology, with $u(0)=u_0$ and $sup_t ||u(t)||_{bmo^{-1}}$ controlled by $||u_0||_{bmo^{-1}} + ||u||_{X_T}^2$.

thm:2.summary: Any global mild solution u in $X_\infty$ to NSE with $u_0$ in $bmo^{-1}$ satisfies $u(t) \to 0$ as t $\to \infty$ in $bmo^{-1} (weak*-topology)$.

rem:1.summary: Theorems 1 and 2 are sharp: heat semigroup is only weak*-continuous on $bmo^{-1}$, and self-similar solutions show the strong topology vanishing fails.

env:prop:L.summary: For f in $L^\infty_{-1}$ cap $T^{\infty,2}_{-1/2}$ cap $T^{\infty,1}$, the operator L(f) belongs to $C_0([0,\infty)$; $bmo^{-1})$ with L(f)(0)=0. The single proposition from which both main theorems follow.

env:prop:L0.summary: For g in $L^\infty_{-1/2}$ cap $T^{\infty,2}$, the maximal regularity operator $L_0(g)$ lies in $C_0([0,\infty)$; $bmo^{-1})$ with $L_0(g)(0)=0$.

env:prop:L1.summary: For f in $T^{\infty,1}$, the remainder operator $L_1(f)$ lies in $C_0([0,\infty)$; $bmo^{-1})$ with $L_1(f)(0)=0$.

lem:1.summary: The operator R(F)(s) := (sDelta)^{-1}(I $- e^{2sDelta}) s^{1/2}$ P Div F(s) is bounded on both $L^\infty_{-1/2}(R^{1+n}_+)$ and $T^{\infty,2}$. Enables the decomposition L $= L_0 + L_1$.

lem:2.summary: For $\phi$ in $DotH^{1,1}$, $sup_t A_t(\phi) \le C||\phi||_{DotH^{1,1}}$ and $A_t(\phi) \to 0$ as t $\to 0+$ and t $\to \infty$. Controls the duality functional used in all three parts of the Prop L0 proof.

lem:3.summary: For $\psi$ satisfying pointwise Gaussian estimates and a in $DotH^{0,1}$, $sup_t integral(G_{3t/4} * |\psi_{t/4} *$ a|^2)^{1/2} dx $\le C||a||_{DotH^{0,1}}$. Proved by Hardy space atomic decomposition.

lem:4.summary: Carleson norm of time-shifted g satisfies $||C^{(2)}_{\tau-\sigma}(tilde{g})||_{L^\infty} \le (\tau-\sigma)^{1/2} \sigma^{-1/2} ||g||_{L^\infty_{-1/2}}$. Controls the iteration remainder terms.

rem:2.summary: Combining the $L^\infty(bmo^{-1})$ bounds from Props L0 and L1 rediscovers the bound of Auscher-Dubois-Tchamitchian, though their original argument is simpler.

edges.detail: Proof of Thm 1 admitting Prop L: applies Prop L to B(u,u) = L(u tensor u) to get weak*-continuity
Proof of Thm 2 admitting Prop L: the $C_0$ part of Prop L gives vanishing at $\infty$
Prop L follows from Prop L0 (maximal regularity part) and Prop L1 (remainder part)
Prop L follows from Prop L0 and Prop L1
Boundedness of R enables the decomposition L $= L_0(R(s^{1/2}f)) + L_1(f)$
All three parts of the Prop L0 proof (continuity at 0, at t>0, long-time limit) use the $A_t$ estimates from Lemma 2
Iteration scheme for $R_1$ uses Lemma 4 to control shifted Carleson norms at each iteration step
Proof of the bound sup $A_t(\phi) \le ||\phi||_{DotH^{1,1}}$ applies Lemma 3 via Gaussian estimates on the heat kernel
Prop L1 uses f in $T^{\infty,1}$ which comes from the decomposition enabled by Lemma 1
Shows Theorem 1 is sharp: heat semigroup is merely weak*-continuous on $bmo^{-1}$
Shows Theorem 2 is sharp: self-similar solutions never vanish in strong topology
Combines $L^\infty(bmo^{-1})$ bounds from Props L0 and L1 to rediscover Auscher-Dubois-Tchamitchian bound
Combines $L^\infty(bmo^{-1})$ bounds from Props L0 and L1

main_results.description: The two main theorems: weak*-continuity in $bmo^{-1}$ and vanishing at $\infty$

key_reduction.description: Single proposition reducing both theorems to a mapping property of the Duhamel operator L on function spaces

operator_decomposition.description: Decomposition L $= L_0 + L_1$ via the bounded operator R, splitting the problem into maximal regularity and remainder

duality_and_iteration.description: Technical core: duality with $DotH^{1,1}$ test functions, $A_t$ functional estimates, and dyadic iteration for the remainder

sharpness_and_context.description: Sharpness results via self-similar solutions and comparison with Auscher-Dubois-Tchamitchian
